question.text: Given USD/JPY = 115.08/115.09 and USD/INR = 75.64/75.65, what is the bid rate for JPY/INR (per 100 JPY)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** To SELL JPY: (1) Sell JPY to get USD — sell at USD/JPY offer = 115.09 (you get 1 USD for 115.09 JPY, so selling 100 JPY gives USD 100/115.09). (2) Sell USD at bid = 75.64 INR. Price of 100 JPY = 75.64/115.09 × 100 = 65.72 INR. **The Trap:** When USD is base in BOTH pairs, DIVIDE instead of multiply. Use offer USD/JPY for JPY/INR bid. **Cross-Exam Context:** JPY/INR cross rate computation is unique to Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** JPY/INR = USD/INR ÷ USD/JPY. For bid: use INR bid ÷ JPY offer. For offer: use INR offer ÷ JPY bid.
